transcript: Fen: You still owe me two days' labour, Rowan. I built your fence and you've done nothing back.
Rowan: I said I'd help with your roof and I will. Winter's not here yet.
Fen: You've been saying that for a month. My roof leaks now.
Rowan: Fine. You've made your point. I'll come by tomorrow and start on it.
Fen: See that you do. I don't like being taken for a fool.
Rowan: You're not. I'll make it right.
ground_truth: A quarrel about an unpaid debt: Fen accuses Rowan of owing him two days' labour for a fence, Rowan admits it and promises to start on Fen's roof tomorrow. Everything stated is grounded in the exchange (the fence was built, the debt is real between them). A faithful gist captures the quarrel and Rowan's promise. No speculation or invented facts; the roof repair is promised, not yet done.
